seoTitle: How to Add Custom Icons to Power BI Reports
metaDescription: Elevate the design of your Power BI reports with custom icons, from finding good sources to importing and applying them cleanly.
intro: Custom icons can make a Power BI report feel more intuitive and visually distinct, especially when default visuals start to feel generic. This guide covers finding good icon sources, importing them into Power BI, and applying them thoughtfully. It's a design upgrade that helps reports stand out while staying easy to understand.

steps.heading: Find custom icons
steps.body: Source high-quality icons that match your report's style and the metrics or sections they'll represent.

steps.heading: Import icons into Power BI
steps.body: Bring the selected icons into your report as images so they can be placed alongside your visuals or on buttons.

steps.heading: Follow best practices for design
steps.body: Keep icon style and sizing consistent across the report so the added visuals feel cohesive rather than mismatched.

steps.heading: Avoid common mistakes
steps.body: Watch out for overusing icons or picking styles that clash with your report's existing branding and color scheme.

steps.heading: Apply icons to enhance clarity
steps.body: Use icons purposefully, such as to differentiate performance metrics or highlight important trends, rather than purely for decoration.

faq.question: Where can I find icons to use in Power BI reports?
faq.answer: Icon sets from reputable icon libraries are commonly used, as long as their licensing allows use in business reports.

faq.question: Do custom icons slow down my Power BI report?
faq.answer: A reasonable number of icons shouldn't noticeably affect performance, though very large image files could add some load time.

faq.question: Can I use icons on buttons as well as static visuals?
faq.answer: Yes, custom icons can be applied to buttons for navigation as well as used as standalone design elements.

faq.question: How do I keep icon style consistent across my report?
faq.answer: Sticking to a single icon set or style family, rather than mixing different visual styles, helps keep the design cohesive.

faq.question: Is adding custom icons difficult for beginners?
faq.answer: It's approachable for beginners, since it mainly involves importing images and placing them like any other visual element.
